title: Failed Break Acceptance Re-Entry
focus_questions: Did the break actually lose outside acceptance?
Was the re-entry back inside old auction accepted or only touched?

summary.thesis: A failed break becomes tradable when outside acceptance fails and the market re-enters and holds inside the prior accepted auction.
summary.operator_translation: Do not fade the first break blindly; wait for failed outside hold and accepted re-entry.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when a break first looks valid but the market returns inside old value or old range and begins rotating there.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: attempted break from a prior accepted area. Required evidence: failed outside hold, re-entry inside old auction, and hold inside the old area. Do not trade: if price is still genuinely holding outside or if re-entry is only a quick flicker. Real trigger: accepted re-entry back into old auction. Failure point: renewed outside acceptance in the original break direction.

when_relevant: A breakout or breakdown first appears valid.
Price re-enters and stabilizes back inside the prior accepted area.
required_evidence: there was a meaningful attempt to trade outside the prior boundary
re-entry back inside the prior auction is visible and held
confirmation_signals: outside trade fails and returns inside the old area
the re-entry back inside is held rather than instantly rejected
follow-through after re-entry shows rotation toward an interior target or opposite edge
invalidation_signals: price regains and holds outside trade in the original break direction
re-entry cannot stabilize inside the old auction
the supposed break never had enough outside trade to matter
no_trade_conditions: Do not fade every first break before outside-hold quality is known.
Do not call the break failed if price has not yet re-entered and held inside the old area.
Do not trade the re-entry if the broader environment is strongly imbalanced and the return inside still looks temporary.

entry_archetypes.name: accepted re-entry
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after price re-enters and holds inside the prior accepted area.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the re-entry is only a brief flicker without hold quality.

entry_archetypes.name: first pullback after re-entry
entry_archetypes.description: Enter on the first controlled pullback after the failed break is already back inside old auction.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the pullback immediately restores the original break direction.

management_notes: The business edge is usually the re-entry, not the first poke outside.
Once inside old auction is accepted, target logic should shift toward interior references.
If re-entry cannot hold, the failed-break read is weaker than it first looked.
review_questions: Was the operator chasing a break that never showed real outside acceptance?
When price re-entered, did it hold inside old value or only flicker back in?
Did the reversal thesis come from true re-entry evidence or from emotional fading?
